pattern: Each element of one array appears in the grand XOR a number of times equal to the other array's length, so parity of the opposite length decides whether that array contributes at all.
hints: Think about how many times a single value from nums1 shows up across all pairings.
An element repeated an even number of times XORs away to zero.
Only when the opposite array has odd length do that array's elements survive, so XOR each array conditionally on the other's parity.

edge_inputs.description: even by even cancels to zero
odd by odd keeps both arrays
one length one preserves it via parity

stress.description: two random arrays of varied parities and large values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    n2 = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    a = [rng.randint(0, 10**9) for _ in range(n1)]
    b = [rng.randint(0, 10**9) for _ in range(n2)]
    return [a, b]
stress.note: random lengths exercise both parity branches
